GOLDSTEIN, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, CHRIS HWANG, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS PRESIDENT, ANN LAZARUS, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS NOTICE OF HEARING OF RESPONDENTS DEMURRER IS CONTINUED TO MAY 16, 2013 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - JUDGE MILLER MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING CRISTINA RUPKE WHO IN AN EMPLOYEE WHERE HIS HUSBAND IS A PARTNER. SHE HAVE MET HER A FEW TIMES OVER THE YEARS THAT SHE WORKED THERE, BUT SHE DOES NOT KNOW HER PERSONALLY. SHE HAVE NEVER TALKED TO HER ABOUT HER SERVICE ON THE MTA COMMISSION OR ABOUT THIS CASE. SHE ALSO DISCLOSED REGARDING ANN LAZARUS IS A SOCIAL FRIEND. SHE SEE HER ABOUT ONCE A YEAR. SHE ASSUMES THAT THIS IS THE SAME ANN LAZARUS WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING IS THE CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS, AND SHE MADE THIS ASSUMPTION ONLY BECAUSE THE ANN LAZARUS THAT SHE KNOWS IS INVOLVED IN CIVIC MATTERS. SHE DID NOT KNOW THIS UNTIL SEEING THE DEMURRER THAT SOMEONE NAMED ANN LAZARUS WAS CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS. SHE NEVER DISCUSEED IT WITH HER. THIS DOES NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. THERE WERE NO OBJECTIONS RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS, CRISTINA RUBKE, SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY BOARD OF APPEALS, CYNTHIA G. GOLDSTEIN, CHRIS HWANG, AND ANN LAZARUS' DEMURRER IS OVERRULED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR THE CITY TO RAISE THIS ISSUE IN ANOTHER CONTEXT. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE CITY HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - JUDGE MILLER MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING CRISTINA RUPKE WHO IN AN EMPLOYEE WHERE HIS HUSBAND IS A PARTNER. SHE HAVE MET HER A FEW TIMES OVER THE YEARS THAT SHE WORKED THERE, BUT SHE DOES NOT KNOW HER PERSONALLY. SHE HAVE NEVER TALKED TO HER ABOUT HER SERVICE ON THE MTA COMMISSION OR ABOUT THIS CASE. SHE ALSO DISCLOSED REGARDING ANN LAZARUS IS A SOCIAL FRIEND. SHE SEE HER ABOUT ONCE A YEAR. SHE ASSUMES THAT THIS IS THE SAME ANN LAZARUS WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING IS THE CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS, AND SHE MADE THIS ASSUMPTION ONLY BECAUSE THE ANN LAZARUS THAT SHE KNOWS IS INVOLVED IN CIVIC MATTERS. SHE DID NOT KNOW THIS UNTIL SEEING THE DEMURRER THAT SOMEONE NAMED ANN LAZARUS WAS CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS. SHE NEVER DISCUSEED IT WITH HER. THIS DOES NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. THERE WERE NO OBJECTIONS RAISED OR QUESTIONS ASKED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Of Respondents Demurrer
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT F. KANE, TEL. NO. 982-1510 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - Judge Miller made a disclosure regarding Cristina Rupke who in an employee where his husband is a partner. She have met her a few times over the years that she worked there, but she does not know her personally. She have never talked to her about her service on the MTA Commission or about this case. She also disclosed regarding Ann Lazarus is a social friend. She see her about once a year. She assumes that this is the same Ann Lazarus whose name appears on the pleading is the chair of the Board of Appeals, and she made this assumption only because the Ann Lazarus that she knows is involved in civic matters. She did not know this until seeing the demurrer that someone named Ann Lazarus was chair of the Board of Appeals. She never discuseed it with her. This does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel opportunity to ask question. There were no objections raised. The matter was argued. RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS, CRISTINA RUBKE, SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY BOARD OF APPEALS, CYNTHIA G. GOLDSTEIN, CHRIS HWANG, and ANN LAZARUS' Demurrer is overruled without prejudice for the city to raise this issue in another context. The court finds that the City has not met its burden. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT F. KANE, TEL. NO. 982-1510 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - Judge Miller made a disclosure regarding Cristina Rupke who in an employee where his husband is a partner. She have met her a few times over the years that she worked there, but she does not know her personally. She have never talked to her about her service on the MTA Commission or about this case. She also disclosed regarding Ann Lazarus is a social friend. She see her about once a year. She assumes that this is the same Ann Lazarus whose name appears on the pleading is the chair of the Board of Appeals, and she made this assumption only because the Ann Lazarus that she knows is involved in civic matters. She did not know this until seeing the demurrer that someone named Ann Lazarus was chair of the Board of Appeals. She never discuseed it with her. This does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel opportunity to ask question. There were no objections raised or questions asked. The matter was argued.
